mechanism_narrative: A downward trend in FEDFUNDS reduces the equity discount rate and supports multiple expansion, so falling policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when rate cuts occur after a growth shock that is already collapsing labor and output.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in a majority of instances where FEDFUNDS has declined over the prior 3 months, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals labor-market deterioration and slower future household income growth, which compresses expected earnings and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next month. The effect is strongest when payroll growth is also soft.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal's out-of-sample hit rate is not below 50% during unemployment upswings.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate, consumers are buying more and firms are hiring more, indicating broad domestic demand strength that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if retail sales rise only because of price inflation rather than real activity.
failure_condition: Reject if joint acceleration in RSAFS and PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-strong instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and better corporate revenue momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is weaker when output growth is driven by inventory rebuilding rather than final demand.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods after the trigger, or if the signal loses predictive power once inventory-driven months are excluded.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged, real policy rates fall and financial conditions ease, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The mechanism fails if inflation rises faster than policy can stay behind it and markets reprice recession risk.
failure_condition: Reject if the spread condition does not coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional rank correlation is indistinguishable from zero over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating PAYEMS trend signals improving labor income and consumption capacity, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when payroll gains are concentrated in low-wage sectors that do not lift aggregate spending.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS momentum does not yield positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the rolling 3-year IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals housing weakness and tighter credit transmission, which reduces consumer confidence and future demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is strongest when the decline is broad rather than weather-driven.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in most cases, or if the signal disappears when weather-affected months are removed.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor and high-demand environment, low UNRATE combined with strong INDPRO indicates sustained real activity without immediate recession risk, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if low unemployment coincides with collapsing output or if industrial production weakens despite labor tightness.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-UNRATE/high-INDPRO condition fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the joint signal underperforms either proxy alone.
